_comment: Two-box LOCAL judge farm for the no-overclaim >=2-family certification gate (RESULTS.md). Box A = DGX Spark serving a Qwen judge via vLLM (CUDA); Box B = Mac Studio serving a Llama judge via MLX (Apple Silicon). Copy to config/inference.local.judge.json and fill in SPARK_HOST / MAC_HOST. Consumed by tools/run_local_judge_eval.py --config and the judge tools' --judges flag.
why_two_boxes: Honest rationale: the >=2-family gate (provenance_bench.aggregate._distinct_families) keys local vLLM/sglang/llamacpp judges by the MODEL vendor, so even two different-vendor models on ONE Spark vLLM port already count as 2 families (qwen + meta-llama). So a second box is NOT required to clear the bar. What the Mac box BUYS: (1) grader INDEPENDENCE — a Metal/MLX runtime is a different numerical + serving stack from CUDA/vLLM, so the two graders' errors are less correlated than two models on the same engine; (2) it OFFLOADS judge compute from the Spark, which is busy training + NVFP4-serving the subject under test; (3) it uses the otherwise-idle Mac. NOTE: do NOT use two SAME-vendor judges (e.g. vllm:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct + vllm:Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct) — both key to 'qwen' = 1 family. (The vendor keying needs the 'vendor/model' slash; a bare 'vllm:Qwen-7B' with no slash would key to 'vllm' instead.)

spark.machine: NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10 Grace Blackwell, aarch64, 128GB unified)
spark.serve: vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct --port 8000   # on the Spark (aarch64+sm_100 build)
spark.notes: vLLM is an AGGREGATOR provider, so this keys to vendor family 'qwen' (not 'vllm'). The Spark also runs the SUBJECT (OLMoE adapter) and the NVFP4 low-RAM serve; keep the judge a DIFFERENT vendor from the subject so judge != subject.

mac.notes: mlx is NOT an aggregator provider, so it keys to family 'mlx' (the engine), regardless of model. Distinct from the Spark judge's 'qwen' family. MLX is Apple-Silicon-only (not usable on the Spark). The Mac is also your control plane: SSH/Remote-SSH into the Spark over Cat 6.

judge_farm._comment: expected_families: how the gate keys these (vLLM->vendor 'qwen', mlx->engine 'mlx'). subject_lineages_to_avoid: do NOT use Qwen or Llama as the SUBJECT under test with these judges (judge != subject). The OLMoE/Sophia-V1 subject (lineage 'allenai'/'olmoe') is clear of both.

gate.notes: These judges supply the >=2-family + kappa>=0.40 inputs; >=3 seeds and CI-excludes-zero are properties of the RUN, not this config.
